title: Implement mu-036: session.start_autonomous + schedule_wakeup
description:
Spec at specs/mu-036-session-autonomous-loop.md (jj spouxlsp d69719c3).

Capability-gated bounded self-driving sessions. server-enforced bounds (max_iterations / max_wall_clock_ms / max_total_tool_calls_in_autonomy), not goodwill-enforced. goal_check_method supports SelfReport (callout), DelegateGrader (another session as oracle — the structural-grader pattern from spec-summary experiment), or ExternalSignal.

schedule_wakeup parks in RunMode::Sleeping consuming no model budget while idle.

Composes with mu-029, mu-031, mu-033, and post-mu-035 status visibility.

Estimated 2 weeks of focused Rust work. Should land after mu-035 since autonomous sessions benefit hugely from provider_status during long unattended runs.

title: Deferred: OS-enforced agent sandboxing for mu
description:
Capture and eventually implement defense-in-depth sandboxing for mu agents after the core framework stabilizes. Design doc: specs/architecture/os-enforced-agent-sandboxing.md

Target architecture:
- minimize/remove bash from production-adjacent profiles
- explicit tools plus tool policy and Biscuit/session capabilities
- trusted supervisor + tiny sandbox-launcher performs pre-cap setup
- Capsicum capability mode before agent-controlled code runs
- Casper/custom brokers for controlled authority and human approval
- jail/pot/ZFS isolation for coarse process/filesystem/network boundaries

Deferred until mu core is mostly working.

title: TUI: implement F8 events explorer
description:
Per mockup, F8 shows raw event log across all sessions with filtering. Reads session.events with kinds_filter.

Useful for debugging and audit trails. v1: list view with scrolling, basic filter by session_id / kind.

title: Verify tool-use rendering in transcript
description:
Ask the model to do something that triggers a tool call (e.g. "read this file" or "glob for *.md"). Verify the magenta `tool: NAME` block renders with:
- args: { ... } line (truncated at 200 chars)
- → ok or !! error result line (truncated at 400 chars)
- result is paired with its call by call_id

If the args/result rendering needs tuning, file follow-ups.

Context:
- render_transcript_lines in crates/mu-tui/src/main.rs pairs ToolCall/ToolResult by call_id
- ToolResult-only rows are skipped (rendered inline under their ToolCall)

title: Implement mu-038 FileBackend — cross-daemon same-machine discovery
description:
Spec at specs/mu-038-projection-queries-and-discovery.md (jj qrpzxpuq 1af4f26a).

FileBackend writes TOML files under ~/.local/share/mu/daemons/<daemon_id>/sessions/<session_id>.toml on announce, deletes on withdraw. session.list with include_remote=true enumerates files across all daemon directories.

Liveness via daemon.heartbeat file mtime — daemons touch it every N seconds. Readers treat sessions from a daemon with stale heartbeat (>30s) as 'may be dead'.

Cross-daemon trust is OUT of scope here — same-user-on-same-machine assumed. Auth comes later (likely biscuit-auth).

title: BUG / external: openai-codex backend stalls — track for awareness
description:
On 2026-05-11 (overnight + into morning) the openai-codex Responses API became unresponsive — >100s with zero wire signal for trivial prompts ('reply with READY').

Token fresh (expires 2026-05-20). Defensive layer surfaces it as MuClientTimeout. Anthropic API works fine in parallel — confirms provider-specific.

Not our bug — but track here so we have a place to note when it recurs / resolves, and so any 'why is codex hung again' question has a single answer.

title: TUI: implement F2 session tree view
description:
Per mu-ui-mockups-2026-05-10.md, F2 shows session lineage as a tree:
  root: mu product discussion
  ├─ impl: mu-022 edit tool        ● run
  │  ├─ delegate: tests            ✓ done
  │  └─ review                     ○ wait
  ├─ design: event context         ✓ done
  └─ design: UI mockups            ● here

session.list returns parent_session_id, so the tree can be built client-side. Render with unicode box-drawing as in the mockup.

title: Cluster-service spec (mu-040): cross-machine federation
description:
Per memory d32898bd. Cross-machine mu visibility goes through a SEPARATE cluster-service process, not via etcd-backed-into-each-daemon.

Service responsibilities:
- Accept client connections (TUI, web UI, agents)
- Authentication / authorization
- Use etcd to enumerate sessions across all daemons in the cluster
- Aggregate cross-daemon mailbox routing

Daemons stay narrow: they just announce themselves + sessions to etcd via a thin SessionDiscovery::EtcdAnnouncerBackend (or similar). The aggregation happens elsewhere.

Future spec — write when we have a real driver for cross-machine work. Likely paired with biscuit-auth for the trust layer.

title: TUI: pagination + scrolling for long transcripts
description:
session.events supports after_event_id pagination. The transcript pane currently pulls limit=500 from event 0 and renders all of it without scrollback. For long sessions (>500 events) we'd miss the end; for very long ones the render would be slow.

Add j/k or PageUp/PageDown to scroll the transcript. Lazy-load via after_event_id when scrolling past the loaded window.

title: TUI: implement F9 mailbox view
description: Per mockup, F9 shows mailbox.post messages received by sessions. Depends on mu-037 phase 1 implementation for the underlying MailboxMessage event + mailbox.list RPC.

title: Implement mu-035: session.provider_status + cancel_outstanding
description:
Spec at specs/mu-035-session-provider-status.md (jj spouxlsp d69719c3).

Three deliverables:
1. session.provider_status periodic notification with kind (awaiting_first_token | streaming | thinking | tool_executing | awaiting_tool_result | idle), started_at_unix_ms, elapsed_ms, optional bytes_received / tool_call_id.
2. session.cancel_outstanding narrow cancel (kills provider call, session stays alive).
3. daemon.outstanding_calls fleet view.

INV-4 is load-bearing: the emit-tick runs on a tokio timer independent of the provider future, so a stalled provider still produces visible status.

Once landed:
- TUI's phase line gets authoritative ground truth (no more session.list-derived heuristics)
- mu_client.py defensive layer becomes belt-and-suspenders
- Refinement experiment's silent stalls become instantly diagnosable

Estimated 1 week of focused Rust work.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text: Phase A landed in jj okqunkry ef544959 — emits ProviderStatus on state transitions (AwaitingFirstToken / Streaming / ToolExecuting). Wire types for cancel_outstanding + daemon.outstanding_calls also added. Remaining: Phase B periodic timer (INV-4 — load-bearing), Phase C session.cancel_outstanding RPC handler, Phase D daemon.outstanding_calls handler.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text: Phase B landed in jj yznoysqw 28ace52b — periodic emit every 1000ms via tokio::time::interval, INV-4 (load-bearing) now actually holds. TUI's phase line can now render 'awaiting first token 4.2s' or 'tool: read 8.1s' with growing elapsed_ms. Phase C (session.cancel_outstanding RPC handler) and Phase D (daemon.outstanding_calls) still to go.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
Phase C landed in jj lmoxwqvs 7733782d — session.cancel_outstanding RPC. AgentInput::CancelOutstanding + Outcome::OutstandingCancelled sentinel; dispatch handler in handle_cancel_outstanding; abort propagates through stream/tool/approval recv sites; outer loop catches and emits Done(Aborted), keeps session alive. was_in field is placeholder until Phase D plumbs the live tracker.

Phase D (daemon.outstanding_calls fleet view) is the last remaining sub-issue — requires promoting the per-session ProviderStatusTracker state into the Sessions registry so dispatch can read it without going through the agent loop's stack frame.

title: Implement mu-037 phase 1: single-daemon peer.hello + mailbox
description:
Spec at specs/mu-037-peer-discovery-mailbox.md (jj ozyprmqn cc380197). Phase 1 only — single-daemon scope.

Two delegate sessions in the same daemon can exchange messages without going through the parent. Wire surface:
- peer.hello / peer.reply with capability advertisement + negotiation
- mailbox.post / mailbox.list / mailbox.consume
- New MailboxMessage event-log entries

Reuses mu-033 Capability entirely — peer handles ARE attenuated capabilities. No new authorization machinery.

Estimated ~1 week. Phase 2 (cross-daemon same-machine via unix socket + filesystem registry) is a follow-up issue.

title: ContextAssembly v2: per-segment source-map
description:
Per specs/architecture/event-sourced-context.md. ContextAssembly v1 records counts + provider info; v2 records the full source-map: for each message in the assembled prompt, which source event(s) it came from and what range.

Enables 'why was this in context' (the F4 Context Inspector view from mu-ui-mockups-2026-05-10.md).

Bigger lift — needs schema work + agent loop participation to actually track the source-event mapping during assembly. Write when the inspector UI work creates real demand.

title: Session persistence across daemon restart
description:
Today sessions live in mu serve's in-memory Sessions map. When mu serve exits (TUI quits → stdin closes → transport exits → sessions drops), every session and its event log are lost.

For a daily-driver TUI that's used across many work sessions, that's a real limitation: you can't 'come back to' the conversation you had yesterday.

What's needed:
  - Event-log persistence (SQLite or JSONL — the event_log.rs design doc references both)
  - SessionDiscovery (mu-038) backend that reads the persisted logs on startup, resurrects SessionInfo entries (the agent loop doesn't need to be re-spawned for a 'cold' session — it spawns on the next ask_session)
  - Cap on retention (per-session age limit? per-session size limit? user choice)
  - Capability state persistence (a session with attenuated capability should keep that on cold reload)

Mentioned as deferred work in mu-036 spec (autonomous loops). Write its own spec when implementation pressure builds — likely after mu-035 Phase B/C/D and mu-037 phase 1 are done.

Workaround for now: keep mu serve running detached; have the TUI connect-to-existing rather than spawn-new. That's not implemented but is a much smaller piece of work than full persistence.

title: Implement mu-037 phase 2: cross-daemon unix-socket peer.hello
description:
Per mu-037 spec phase 2. Adds mu serve --listen <path> flag so daemons can accept peer connections from other daemons on the same machine.

Builds on mu-038's FileBackend for discovery (find the peer's socket path via the filesystem registry).

Depends on mu-037 phase 1 (peer.hello + mailbox primitives) and mu-038 FileBackend.

title: TUI: suppress empty 'assistant' block on tool-only turns
description:
Currently when the model emits a turn that is entirely tool calls (no text content blocks), the transcript renders a 'assistant — (no text in this turn)' block above the magenta tool block. That's cosmetic cruft.

Fix in crates/mu-tui/src/main.rs::render_transcript_lines: when an 'assistant_message_event' has empty joined text AND there's a 'tool_call' or 'tool_result' event in the same logical turn (i.e. next significant event in the log is a tool_call before the next done), skip rendering the empty assistant block entirely. The tool block itself is the visible record of what the model did this turn.

Discovered in mu-7n3 verify screenshot (2026-05-11).

title: Metrics framework: per-call time decomposition + quality proxies
description:
Design + implementation arc for mu's ongoing observability. Came out of refinement-experiment discussion 2026-05-11: per-request hard metrics vs per-request quality metrics are different beasts, and we should be deliberate about which we track and why.

Captured in commit/results doc: refinement arm 0/3 forbidden vs baseline 3/3, at 2.2x wall time. That cost is fine for delegation-style work but expensive for interactive — we want to be able to SEE the cost decomposition (TTFT, streaming, tool time, thinking) rather than just total wall.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Two classes of metrics, very different to track
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Hard metrics (always trackable, no ground truth):
  - TTFT = AwaitingFirstToken → Streaming transition (mu-035)
  - Streaming duration
  - ToolExecuting duration (sum)
  - Wall end-to-end
  - Tokens in/out, cache hit %, tool count
  - Per-provider error rate

These don't tell us correctness but they DO catch drift: median TTFT per provider per hour would catch 'codex is stalled' in minutes instead of the 14h silence we had on 2026-05-10/11.

Quality metrics (need fixture):
  - Success rate vs ground truth
  - Refinement passes to clean
  - Cost-to-correct = total tokens to 1 successful response

These need a benchmark fixture (like the spec-summary task in protocol-eval). Outside fixtures, every request is unique.

Proxy metrics (work without ground truth):
  - Refinement-fire rate (if we run grader on everything)
  - User-marked retry rate (TUI accept/reject affordance)
  - Tool error rate (model-induced vs system-induced)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Implementation arc
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Now-easy (post mu-035 A/B/C + mu-038):
  - daemon.usage_history projection RPC returning median/p95 of
    TTFT / streaming_duration / tool_duration / wall, grouped by
    (provider, model, time-bucket).
  - Backed by aggregating ProviderStatus events from the event log
    (or live tracker snapshots once tracker is in Sessions — Phase D).

Soonish (mu-xln existing P2 bead — F5 Usage Dashboard):
  - Renders the daemon.usage_history projection.
  - Per-provider/model rows with TTFT p95, streaming duration p95,
    tool-call-count distribution.
  - Cost estimate column once we have a per-provider price table.

Later (quality proxies):
  - TUI affordance: '👍 helpful' / '👎 not what I wanted' / 'try again'
    after each session.done.
  - Capture in event log (new EventPayload variant: UserFeedback).
  - Surface as a quality-proxy column in F5.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Cross-references
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Depends on:
  - mu-iwq Phase D (daemon.outstanding_calls + ProviderStatusTracker
    in Sessions) — for snapshot enumeration. Phase A/B/C already
    give us the per-call event stream.
  - mu-038 projection-query patterns — same code area, same style.

Composes with:
  - mu-xln (F5 dashboard) — its data source.
  - mu-036 autonomous loop — refinement-fire-rate is a great
    quality proxy for autonomous sessions.

Not in scope here:
  - Cost mapping (provider+model → $/1M tokens). Should be its
    own small bead because it's an updated-rarely lookup table.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

When to implement: not blocking daily-driver. Worth picking up
after mu-iwq Phase D lands or as a parallel slice if a slow
provider catches our attention again.

comments.author: tcovert
comments.text:
Per-model corrections rate is a great proxy metric to add to the design — break down refinement_fire_rate by (provider, model) and you get a comparative quality fingerprint. Different models have different priors (today's experiment confirmed gpt-5.5 has a strong login/logout prior; haiku-4.5 may have different ones). Tracking corrections_per_call by model surfaces:
  - which models are best for which task types (factual extraction, code review, etc.)
  - whether a model is degrading over time (vendor pushed an update? prior drift?)
  - cost-of-correctness tradeoff (cheaper-but-more-corrections vs more-expensive-but-fewer)

Add to the F5 dashboard sketch: per-(provider, model) row showing corrections_per_100_calls when grader was enabled. Surfaces 'haiku gets 5/100 wrong on summarisation; gpt-5.5 gets 33/100; sonnet gets 2/100' or whatever the truth turns out to be.

title: Re-run refinement-loop experiment when openai-codex is healthy
description:
Pre-registered H4/H5/H6 are at ~/src/claude-personal/research/protocol-eval/docs/predictions.md (2026-05-11 section).

Blocker: openai-codex backend stalled >100s with zero wire signal as of 2026-05-11 ~10am. Token is fresh (expires 2026-05-20), defensive layer surfaces it as MuClientTimeout('session.done got ack, awaiting terminal') — so when codex comes back, the experiment will fail-fast if it fails at all.

To re-run:
  cd ~/src/claude-personal/research/protocol-eval
  uv run --project . python3 scripts/run_mu_delegation.py --arm refinement --trials 3

Then write up results in results/delegation_spec_summary_2026-05-11.md (extend the existing Refinement section).

H4: passed >= 2/3 (vs baseline 0/3)
H5: median refinement passes <= 2
H6: required-fact coverage >= 85% aggregate

title: BUG: session loop terminates after first ask_session — multi-turn broken
description:
Found during mu-s6r verification (2026-05-11). First ask completes cleanly, but the second ask returns:
  mu serve error on ask_session: { code: -32603, message: 'session loop has terminated' }

Expected: a session keeps its agent loop running, processes successive ask_session calls in order.

Looking at crates/mu-core/src/agent/loop_.rs run(): likely the loop returns after processing one user message instead of looping back to wait for the next.

This blocks daily-driver use — every workflow involves more than one turn. Priority P1.

title: Verify multi-turn TUI flow end-to-end
description:
From F3 transcript view, press i and send another prompt to the selected session. Verify:
- The new user message appends as a fresh `you` block below the previous done terminator
- The yellow streaming block appears for the new response
- It transitions cleanly to a white assistant block on session.done
- Earlier conversation history stays visible above

Smoke this manually; if it works, mark closed. If it doesn't, file specific bugs.

Context:
- jj lxrylpvz 9534bf97 has the transcript-parse fix
- mu-tui's i shortcut is bound in on_key_normal so works from any view

title: Update rustls-webpki to fix 3 dependabot alerts (1 high, 2 low)
description:
GitHub dependabot flagged 3 alerts on main (all rustls-webpki, transitive via reqwest → rustls):

  high  GHSA-82j2-j2ch-gfr8  DoS panic on malformed CRL BIT STRING
  low   GHSA-xgp8-3hg3-c2mh  Wildcard name constraints accepted
  low   GHSA-965h-392x-2mh5  URI name constraints incorrectly accepted

Real risk for mu: modest. We talk TLS outbound to anthropic.com / chatgpt.com / openrouter.ai (well-known services). We don't process CRLs ourselves. High-severity DoS would need a malicious server in the chain.

Fix: cargo update -p rustls-webpki. Test the workspace still builds + tests pass. P3 because workaround = trusted upstreams.

title: Refresh ground_truth.json with mu-035/036/037/038
description:
All 3 refinement trials showed extras=4 because four new specs (mu-035, mu-036, mu-037, mu-038) were authored 2026-05-11 after the ground_truth.json was frozen. The model correctly listed them; the grader flagged them as 'extras' because they weren't in expected_specs.

Update ~/src/claude-personal/research/protocol-eval/tasks/delegation/spec-summary/ground_truth.json to include the four new specs' required_facts / bonus_facts / forbidden_claims. Then a re-run of any arm should show extras=0 by default.

Without this fix, the strict passed criterion stays 0/3 even though substantive H4 is confirmed (forbidden_total=0).

title: TUI color polish — moonfly/github_dark family
description:
Reference memory 269e44ce (user color preferences). Current TUI uses some saturated colors (yellow streaming block, bright Green status) that feel slightly louder than the moonfly/github_dark aesthetic Thaddeus prefers.

Pass:
- Yellow Color::Yellow for streaming block / awaiting-first-token → muted teal or soft amber
- Green Color::Green for running session glyph → cooler/desaturated
- Active-tab Black-on-Cyan is closest to the editors and can stay
- Budget warning red threshold can stay (signals real danger)

Test by running TUI alongside helix open in another terminal — colors should feel of-a-piece, not jarring.

title: biscuit-auth integration spec — capability tokens across daemons
description:
Once mu-037 phase 2+ enables cross-daemon work, we need an auth model. The biscuit-auth / macaroon tradition argued for in specs/architecture/capability-delegation.md is the obvious direction.

Open questions:
- Issuer model (each daemon as a CA? a central authority?)
- Revocation (mu has no revocation primitive today)
- Capability serialization wire format
- Composition with mu-033 Capability — biscuit IS a capability, not a separate auth concept

Write the spec when implementation pressure arrives; for now this is a placeholder.

title: BUG: orphan mu serve / silent Popen hang under background-bash spawning
description:
Memory a1dd04fa has the full diagnostic pattern.

Symptom: Python orchestrator using MuClient hangs forever on fresh ask. No mu serve child appears in process tree; python sits in I (idle) syscall-state with ~0 CPU. No stderr. No exception.

Reproduces under Bash run_in_background=true especially with stale mu serve processes around. Foreground works.

Suspected mechanism: subprocess.Popen fork window stretching indefinitely under background-bash supervision. Possibly compounded by file-lock contention on ~/.config/mu/auth/openai-codex.json across daemons.

Workaround SHIPPED: mu_client.py defensive layer (jj qmlywmnm 186d5970, claude-personal scripts). read_timeout + on_progress + stderr drainer + orphan warning. Failures now surface as MuClientTimeout instead of silent stalls.

Real fix unclear without more diagnosis. Lower priority because workaround makes the failure visible rather than mysterious — but worth chasing when bandwidth allows.

title: TUI: implement F5 usage / cache dashboard
description:
Per mockup, F5 shows cumulative usage by provider/model, cost estimate, cache hit %, and a cache ledger panel.

Cumulative usage already exposed via daemon.stats (total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens). Per-provider breakdown needs a session.list grouped/aggregated client-side OR a new daemon.usage_by_provider RPC. Start with client-side aggregation; promote to RPC if needed.

Cost estimation needs a per-provider price table — track as a separate issue.
